pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 29 checks port 6661; export_result.sh accepts connections on 6661
setup_task.sh line 19 creates directory; export_result.sh line 39 checks for output files
setup_task.sh lines 36-41 creates test message at this path

HL7v2 test message PID segment field 3 (Patient ID): 'MRN12345^^^MAIN_HOSP^MR~987654321^^^SSA^SS' - first component is MRN12345. PID-3 is the correct field per HL7v2 specification for Patient Identifier List. See https://www.hl7.eu/refactored/segPID.html

HL7v2 test message PID segment field 5 (Patient Name): 'SMITH^JOHN^MICHAEL^^MR' - family name is first component. PID-5 is correct per HL7v2 specification for Patient Name (XPN). See https://www.hl7.eu/refactored/segPID.html
HL7v2 test message PID segment field 5: 'SMITH^JOHN^MICHAEL^^MR' - given name is second component

HL7v2 test message PID segment field 7 (Date/Time of Birth): '19780523' converted to YYYY-MM-DD format. PID-7 is correct per HL7v2 specification. See https://www.hl7.eu/refactored/segPID.html. FHIR R4 requires ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD). See https://www.hl7.org/fhir/datatypes.html

HL7v2 test message PID segment field 8 (Administrative Sex): 'M' mapped to 'male'. PID-8 is correct per HL7v2 specification. FHIR R4 Patient resource accepts gender codes: male | female | other | unknown. See https://www.hl7.org/fhir/R4/patient.html

HL7v2 test message PID segment field 11 (Patient Address): '123 OAK STREET^^CHICAGO^IL^60601^US' - city is third component. PID-11 is correct per HL7v2 specification for Patient Address (XAD). See https://www.hl7.eu/refactored/segPID.html
HL7v2 test message PID segment field 11: '123 OAK STREET^^CHICAGO^IL^60601^US' - state is fourth component
HL7v2 test message PID segment field 11: '123 OAK STREET^^CHICAGO^IL^60601^US' - zip is fifth component

HL7v2 test message PID segment field 13 (Phone Number - Home): '3125551234^PRN^PH' - phone number is first component. PID-13 is correct per HL7v2 specification. See https://www.hl7.eu/refactored/segPID.html

pi_items.metadata_value: MLLP with 0x0B start, 0x1C end, 0x0D carriage return
pi_items.source: export_result.sh lines 50-61 use correct MLLP framing. Per HL7 standard, MLLP frame structure is: 0x0B (Start Block/VT), data, 0x1C (End Block/FS), 0x0D (CR). See https://www.hl7.eu/refactored/transport01mllp.html and https://hl7.org/implement/standards/product_brief.cfm?product_id=55

HL7v2 test message MSH segment: 'MSH|^~\&|EPIC|MAIN_HOSP|FHIR_GW|DEST_SYS|20240115120000||ADT^A01^ADT_A01|MSG00001|P|2.5.1|||AL|NE'. ADT^A01 is 'Admit/visit notification' trigger event per HL7v2 specification. See https://www.hl7.eu/refactored/msgADT_A01.html
HL7v2 test message MSH segment field 12: '2.5.1'

task.json requirement mentions 'FHIR R4 Patient JSON resources' in description. FHIR R4 is the primary FHIR release version with complete Patient resource specification at https://www.hl7.org/fhir/R4/patient.html

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic test data from a programmatically generated HL7v2 ADT^A01 message. The test patient is 'SMITH, JOHN' (MRN: MRN12345) born 1978-05-23 with address in Chicago, IL and phone 312-555-1234 (note: 555 exchange is reserved for fictional numbers). All HL7v2 field references (PID-3, PID-5, PID-7, PID-8, PID-11, PID-13) are correct per HL7v2 specification. All expected FHIR R4 mappings are valid per the official FHIR specification: identifier (from PID-3), name.family/given (from PID-5), birthDate in ISO 8601 format (from PID-7), gender codes (from PID-8), address components (from PID-11), and telecom (from PID-13). The MLLP transport framing (0x0B + message + 0x1C + 0x0D) is correctly implemented per HL7 standards.
